lines.text: DC actually OWNS a character that Marvel made famous, and they only charge one dollar a year to use him.
lines.broll: Captain Marvel Shazam comic book covers

lines.text: Back in the 1940s, Fawcett Comics created a superhero called Captain Marvel.
DC sued them into oblivion, claiming he was a Superman ripoff.
DC bought the character, but here is the twist.
While DC was busy suing, Marvel swooped in and trademarked the name Captain Marvel.
lines.broll: Marvel Captain Marvel character Carol Danvers

lines.text: Now DC has to call their guy Shazam instead.
Marvel pays DC just one dollar annually to keep using that name.
DC literally owns the original but cannot even use his real name.
lines.broll: Side by side Shazam and Captain Marvel

broll_cues.search_query: Shazam Captain Marvel comic book vintage

outro_line: DC literally owns the original but cannot even use his real name.
narration: DC actually OWNS a character that Marvel made famous, and they only charge one dollar a year to use him. His name is Captain Marvel. Back in the 1940s, Fawcett Comics created a superhero called Captain Marvel. DC sued them into oblivion, claiming he was a Superman ripoff. DC bought the character, but here is the twist. While DC was busy suing, Marvel swooped in and trademarked the name Captain Marvel. Now DC has to call their guy Shazam instead. Marvel pays DC just one dollar annually to keep using that name. DC literally owns the original but cannot even use his real name.

_topic.title: Marvel Paid DC $1 to Use a Character
_topic.hook: DC actually OWNS a character that Marvel made famous, and they only charge one dollar a year to use him.
_topic.angle: The bizarre legal history of Captain Marvel/Shazam creates a mind-blowing corporate rivalry twist most fans never knew existed.
